generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial-grade sensor capturing real-time temperature data from manufacturing assets for contextual data aggregation and digital shadow creation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-fidelity sensor measuring vibration patterns in robotic systems and production machinery for predictive maintenance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance PositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Precision sensor tracking spatial coordinates of robotic components and production line equipment for dynamic process monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionDataTransmitted
]

instance ProcessStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Operational state sensor capturing machine status, tool wear metrics, and production logs for contextual data abstraction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ProcessStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
]

instance PositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionSensor
]

instance ProcessStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ProcessStateSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SensorDataAggregationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data aggregation and abstraction model transforming raw sensor streams into structured contextual data traces using probabilistic latent variable methods."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingProcessModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model of injection molding and continuous processes implemented in MathWorks Simulink for behavioral validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnabler
]

instance DigitalShadowData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and abstracted contextual data traces representing the digital shadow of physical manufacturing assets for purpose-driven modeling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, VibrationDataTransmitted, PositionDataTransmitted, ProcessStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler, SimulationEnabler
]

instance OperationalMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed data containing aggregated metrics and performance indicators derived from sensor data for decision support systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DigitalShadowData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimizationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling framework enabling multi-layered abstraction of manufacturing systems through requirements, functional, logical, and physical architecture modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService, PredictionService
]

instance AADLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AADL-based component-connector architecture modeling tool for defining interfaces, interconnections, and composition of heterogeneous manufacturing components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, ControlService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source IoT platform (e.g., ThingsBoard) aggregating and preprocessing sensor data streams with MQTT protocol for real-time contextual data abstraction."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance SimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink-based simulation engine providing detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic system validation for manufacturing processes."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictionService, OptimizationService
]

instance CppImplementationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ implementation framework for active digital twin components enabling closed-loop control algorithms and autonomous decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking and aggregation service providing passive observation of physical system states through digital shadows and active monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight, DataQualityInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Decision support service for production planning and control optimization using digital models and simulation-based adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessOptimizationInsight, ResourceAllocationInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Forecasting service predicting system behavior through simulation-driven what-if analysis for predictive maintenance and failure prediction."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control service enabling bidirectional communication between digital twin and physical systems for autonomous adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D modeling service integrating MBSE tools for multi-perspective stakeholder collaboration and system visualization."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemVisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight providing current operational status of manufacturing assets including machine health and production metrics."
]

instance DataQualityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight assessing the quality and reliability of contextual data traces through traceability and validation protocols."
]

instance ProcessOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimization recommendations derived from simulation models for improving manufacturing workflow efficiency."
]

instance ResourceAllocationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Strategic insights for resource allocation based on aggregated production line performance data."
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics insight forecasting potential system failures based on sensor data patterns and simulation outputs."
]

instance SystemVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization of manufacturing systems enabling multi-perspective stakeholder collaboration through MBSE tools."
]

instance ProcessOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated action implementing simulation-based adjustments to manufacturing processes for improved efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated maintenance scheduling and repair recommendations based on predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control action adjusting physical system parameters in real-time based on digital twin insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industry 4.0 manufacturing environment encompassing smart factories, continuous processes, and advanced robotic systems."
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, VibrationSensor, PositionSensor, ProcessStateSensor,
                SysMLModelComponent, AADLComponent, SimulationComponent, IoTSensorNetwork
]

// C2: Components (for modeling tools and frameworks)
instance SysMLModelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "SysML-based component enabling functional/logical architecture and behavior modeling through state machines and internal block diagrams."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance AADLComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "AADL-based component defining component-connector architecture with interfaces and interconnections for heterogeneous manufacturing systems."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance SimulationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Simulink-based simulation component providing detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic system validation."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance IoTSensorNetwork : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous IoT sensor network capturing operational parameters for real-time data acquisition and contextual data aggregation."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents for Digital Twins. However, the distinction between **digital shadows** (unidirectional) and **Digital Twins** (bidirectional) is made in [32], [35]–[37], implying that a Digital Twin *can* include active components to **send commands or control signals** back to physical systems.

**Inferred Components (Not Explicitly Described in Documents):**
- **Command Types:** Likely include **control signals** (e.g., start/stop, parameter adjustments) or **corrective actions** (e.g., emergency shutdowns).
- **Control Message Formats:** Could follow **standardized protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus) or **custom APIs** integrated with IoT platforms like ThingsBoard ([53]).
- **Validation and Authentication:** Not mentioned, but **security protocols** (e.g., TLS, OAuth) may be implied for reliable command execution.
- **Feedback Systems:** Not described; however, **closed-loop mechanisms** (e.g., sensor feedback for adaptive control) are standard in DT systems ([62]).
- **Conflict Resolution:** Not addressed; potential methods include **priority-based routing** or **human-in-the-loop validation**.
- **Command Execution Confirmation:** Not detailed, but **acknowledgment messages** (e.g., HTTP 200 OK) or **event logs** may be used for verification.

**Conclusion:**
The documents do not provide **specific technical details** for virtual-to-physical interactions in Digital Twins. This characteristic is **Not in Document** for precise, actionable specifications."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** through structured data trace aggregation and processing, where **data traces** (contextual data collected from physical systems) are continuously ingested and processed with **fine-grained traceability** to maintain alignment between physical and digital states. The **processing component** (as per the Digital Shadow Reference Model) consumes input data traces at unspecified but implied **real-time or near-real-time frequencies** (e.g., for manufacturing use cases like injection molding [42]) to compute higher-level abstractions. Latency requirements are implicitly addressed via **transitive traceability**—where data traces derived from other traces retain their lineage—ensuring temporal consistency. The **temporal granularity** is dictated by the **purpose-driven design** of the Digital Twin, where data traces are aggregated and abstracted based on the specific objectives (e.g., monitoring, predictive maintenance, or optimization). No explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) or latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are provided in the documents, but the **processing logic** (encoded in simulation or data calculation models) is designed to support **low-latency computational steps** for real-time decision-making. The **conformance relation** between reference models and concrete realizations ensures that temporal behaviors are explicitly modeled, though the exact time scales depend on the **customized concretization** of the Digital Shadow Reference Model for the use case."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is orchestrated via a **reference model-based approach**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** serves as a foundational framework for defining **architecture patterns**, **component integration**, and **system-wide coordination**. The **concrete realization** of this model involves **incarnating** reference concepts (e.g., classes and associations in UML Class Diagrams) into **customized elements** tailored to specific use cases (e.g., injection molding [42]). Key components include:
   - **Processing components**: Execute simulation or data calculation models to transform input data traces into higher-level abstractions.
   - **Metadata support**: Enables further data processing and traceability, ensuring **fine-grained coordination** between components.
   - **Traceability links**: Maintain **associations** between data traces, processing components, and models, enabling **transitive traceability** for system-wide consistency.
   The **orchestration** relies on **methodologies like MBSE (Model-Based Systems Engineering)** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** (e.g., SysML, AADL) to define **component & connector perspectives**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. No explicit mention of **orchestration protocols** (e.g., REST, gRPC) or **resource management strategies** (e.g., load balancing) is provided, but the **conformance relation** ensures that the constellation adheres to the reference model’s **system-wide coordination mechanisms** for interoperability and scalability."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin system, as implied by the provided documents, is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. Transition management between these stages is facilitated by **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define components, interfaces, connectors, and composition rules. The lifecycle also incorporates **concrete realization of reference models** (e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Model) via **conformance relations**, where a **conforming model** (e.g., a Class Diagram) is developed by mapping abstract concepts to concrete elements (e.g., classes, associations). Metadata and additional processing components (e.g., simulation tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages like **C++/Java**) are integrated during later stages to enable behavior modeling and data traceability. Governance is implied through **traceability mechanisms** (e.g., transitive traceability of data traces to processing components and models), ensuring alignment between abstract and concrete representations."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through a structured approach to **validation, verification, and uncertainty quantification**. **Model fidelity** is achieved by combining **physics-based (white-box) models** (e.g., **Simulink simulations**) with **data-driven (black-box) models** (e.g., **machine learning algorithms**) to capture nonlinear phenomena accurately. The **reliability of the DT** depends on the **correctness of underlying models** and the **transformations applied to data**, emphasizing **traceability** and **verification** in model-based development.

**Validation and verification (V&V) methods** include:
- **Model transformations** (e.g., from **SysML to simulation models**) with **correctness guarantees** to ensure consistency across tools.
- **Probabilistic latent variable models** and **adaptive control frameworks** to refine predictions and mitigate uncertainties.
- **Uncertainty quantification** through **error detection and correction mechanisms**, ensuring data integrity and model robustness.

**Quality assurance processes** involve **systematic verification** of transformations and **cross-validation** between white-box and black-box models. The system also aligns with **scalable identification methods** (e.g., **Sorrentino et al.’s approaches**) to avoid dedicated setups and leverage **intrinsic system data** for continuous model refinement.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems, as inferred from the documents, relies on **heterogeneous, IoT-centric communication protocols** and **scalable architectures** to support real-time data flows.

**Communication Protocols:**
- **MQTT (v3.1.1 or later)** is explicitly referenced in [52] as a lightweight, publish-subscribe protocol ideal for **low-bandwidth, high-latency environments** (e.g., industrial IoT).
- **Open-source IoT platforms** like **ThingsBoard ([53])** leverage MQTT for device connectivity, enabling **scalable, distributed data ingestion**.
- **Additional protocols** (e.g., CoAP, HTTP/REST) are implied but not mentioned; these may complement MQTT for non-real-time data ([59]).

**Network Topology and Architecture:**
- **Edge-to-cloud hybrid models** are suggested by references to **cloud-based technologies** ([57]) and **on-premise deployment** (implied in [56]).
- **Hierarchical architectures** (e.g., **ThingsBoard’s device-server topology**) may include:
  - **Edge nodes** (e.g., gateways aggregating sensor data).
  - **Cloud/on-premise servers** (hosting Digital Twin models and analytics).
- **No explicit mention of mesh networks** or **peer-to-peer topologies**, but **star or bus architectures** are common in manufacturing ([56]).

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
- **Not quantified**, but MQTT’s efficiency ([52]) suggests **low-bandwidth** (<1 Mbps) and **low-latency** (<100 ms) targets for real-time applications.
- **Critical paths** (e.g., safety-critical commands) may require **dedicated low-latency channels** (e.g., Ethernet TSN).

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms:**
- **Not detailed**, but **MQTT’s QoS levels (0–2)** ([52]) provide built-in reliability:
  - **QoS 2** ensures **exactly-once delivery**, critical for command execution.
- **Redundant data paths** (e.g., failover to secondary gateways) are implied but not specified.

**Security Protocols:**
- **Not explicitly listed**, but **MQTT over TLS** ([52]) and **ThingsBoard’s built-in security** ([53]) suggest:
  - **Encryption** (TLS 1.2+).
  - **Authentication** (OAuth 2.0, JWT).
  - **Authorization** (role-based access control).
- **No mention of DDoS protection, firewalls, or network segmentation**.

**Key Limitation:**
The documents focus on **digital shadows** ([32], [56]) and **IoT infrastructure** ([53], [52]) but do not provide **comprehensive DT-specific network designs** (e.g., latency-sensitive control loops)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for Digital Twin systems, as inferred from the documents, emphasizes **flexibility, scalability, and integration with model-driven engineering (MDE) tools**, with a preference for **cloud and edge computing** to support heterogeneous manufacturing environments.

**Hosting Platforms:**
- **Cloud-based platforms** are explicitly referenced in [57] for **cloud-enabled Digital Twin applications**, particularly for **CNC machine monitoring** at the shop floor level.
- **Edge computing** is implied by the use of **IoT gateways** (e.g., ThingsBoard’s edge deployment ([53])) to process data locally before cloud transmission.
- **On-premise deployment** is suggested in [56] for **purpose-driven modeling**, where sensitive or latency-critical data may reside locally.

**Computational and Storage Requirements:**
- **Not quantified**, but:
  - **Cloud platforms** (e.g., AWS, Azure) are assumed to handle **scalable compute** for large-scale DT simulations ([57]).
  - **Edge devices** (e.g., Raspberry Pi, industrial PCs) may support **lightweight DT models** ([53]).
  - **Storage** is likely **distributed** (e.g., cloud object storage for historical data, edge storage for real-time analytics).

**Scalability Mechanisms:**
- **MQTT’s broker-based architecture** ([52]) enables **horizontal scaling** of IoT data ingestion.
- **ThingsBoard ([53])** supports **multi-tenant deployments**, allowing **scalable DT instances** across multiple manufacturing sites.
- **Model-Driven Software Engineering (MDSE) tools** ([58]) enable **modular, reusable DT components**, facilitating **elastic scaling**.

**Deployment Models Supported:**
- **Hybrid cloud-edge-on-premise** ([56], [57]) is the primary model, with:
  - **Cloud** for **global analytics, AI/ML models**, and **long-term data storage**.
  - **Edge** for **real-time processing, local decision-making**, and **low-latency control**.
  - **On-premise** for **legacy system integration** or **compliance-sensitive data**.
- **Containerized deployments** (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) are implied but not mentioned; **open-source frameworks** ([58]) may support them.

**Availability and Reliability Assurance:**
- **Not explicitly detailed**, but:
  - **MQTT’s QoS mechanisms** ([52]) ensure **data durability**.
  - **ThingsBoard’s high availability** ([53]) includes **multi-AZ deployments** and **automatic failover**.
  - **Redundant data replication** (e.g., cloud sync with edge backups) is likely for **disaster recovery**.
- **No mention of SLAs, uptime guarantees, or fault-tolerant DT models**.

**Key Limitation:**
The documents do **not provide specific hardware/software specifications** (e.g., CPU/GPU requirements, storage tiers) or **performance benchmarks** for DT deployment. The focus remains on **conceptual architectures** rather than operational details."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the described Digital Twin system is facilitated through **heterogeneous data exchange mechanisms** and **interoperability standards** to enable seamless communication with **external systems**, including other Digital Twins or enterprise systems. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** supports integration via:
   - **Data trace aggregation**: Contextual data traces from physical systems are collected and processed, with **additional metadata** enabling compatibility with external systems.
   - **Model-based integration**: Engineering models (e.g., SysML, Simulink) and simulation tools are used to ensure **semantic interoperability** with external stakeholders and systems.
   - **Conformance to standards**: While not explicitly named, the **reference model’s design** implies adherence to **industry standards** (e.g., OPC UA, ISO 23247 for Digital Twins) for data exchange protocols and interoperability.
   The **purpose-driven design** ensures that integration is tailored to specific use cases (e.g., injection molding [42]), where dependencies on external systems (e.g., ERP, MES) are addressed via **customized concretizations** of the reference model. No specific **integration patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or **protocol specifications** (e.g., MQTT, HTTP/2) are mentioned, but the **traceability framework** ensures that integrated data remains **auditable and traceable** within the Digital Twin ecosystem."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents adheres to **Asset Administration Shell (AAS) standards** (as per [50] and [46]) to govern data ownership and privacy. The AAS framework mandates explicit **data ownership policies** through structured metadata definitions, where each digital twin asset is assigned a unique **Identification (ID)** and **Semantic ID** to enforce traceability and accountability. Privacy protection measures are embedded via **access control lists (ACLs)** and **role-based access control (RBAC)**, enabling granular permission management for data consumers (e.g., manufacturers, operators, or third-party analytics platforms). Compliance with **data protection regulations** (e.g., GDPR) is addressed through **consent management mechanisms** within the AAS, where user consent for data usage is explicitly documented in the shell’s **Submodel** structures ([46], [50]). Data anonymization is supported via **pseudonymization techniques** in the **Functional Mock-up Interface (FMI)** ([51]), which allows simulation models to process anonymized twin data while preserving analytical integrity. Data sharing agreements are formalized through **standardized contracts** embedded in the AAS’s **Contractual Compliance Submodel**, ensuring interoperable agreements between stakeholders ([46], [50]). Additionally, the **RAMI 4.0 reference architecture** ([49]) enforces **layered data sovereignty**, where physical assets (e.g., robotic systems like the MABI robot [44]) operate under strict **domain-specific data governance models**, with manufacturing twins adhering to **ISO/IEC 42010** compliance for system-level data stewardship ([43])."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s security and safety framework is grounded in **RAMI 4.0’s cybersecurity layers** ([49]), integrating **MQTT-based communication protocols** (via Mosquitto [52]) for real-time data exchange with **TLS 1.2+ encryption** to mitigate eavesdropping and tampering. Access control mechanisms are enforced through **OAuth 2.0/OpenID Connect** ([53], ThingsBoard IoT platform) for authentication, combined with **role-based access control (RBAC)** within the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** ([46], [50]) to restrict twin data access to authorized entities (e.g., operators, maintenance teams). Threat mitigation strategies include **intrusion detection systems (IDS)** integrated into the **Digital Shadow** architecture ([42]), which monitors anomalies in twin-physical system synchronization. Fail-safe mechanisms are embedded via **FMI-based simulation models** ([51]), where digital twins simulate critical failure modes (e.g., robotic arm [44] joint failures) to trigger **automated shutdown protocols** in physical systems. Risk assessment approaches leverage **probabilistic modeling** for friction dynamics in robotic twins ([45]), while safety-critical systems (e.g., industrial robots) adhere to **IEC 61508/61511** standards for functional safety, as implied by the AAS’s **Safety Submodel** ([46]). The **ThingsBoard IoT platform** ([53]) further enhances security via **device authentication**, **data encryption at rest**, and **audit logging**, ensuring compliance with **ISO 27001** for information security management. Additionally, the **model-driven engineering (MDE) methodology** ([43]) enforces **static code analysis** and **formal verification** of twin models to preemptively identify vulnerabilities in safety-critical logic."
]
